scope: runtime/live-proof tracker for the current-target supported widget set
claim: This matrix tracks the live-proof gap after the all-supported-controls offline build audit. It does not claim every supported widget has COM36 runtime proof.

status_values.committed-live-proof: A committed hardware evidence JSON or equivalent result artifact records a live COM36 run for this widget or its narrow behavior family.
status_values.documented-live-proof: Repository docs claim live proof, but this matrix did not find a dedicated committed result JSON for that widget in this pass.
status_values.smoke-ready: The scene has a narrow live smoke recipe or expectation file, but no committed successful live result is claimed here.
status_values.offline-only: The widget is covered by writer/offline clean rebuild evidence only.

not_claimed: live COM36 proof for every widget
speaker or codec validation for audio
successful playback for every video encoding
physical touch lockout proof from serial click

animation.next_live_action: Build a resource-backed single GMOV TFT, upload with live_tft_smoke, enable gm0.en, and capture camera proof that the animation moves.

audio.next_live_action: Run the SD-audio smoke and start/stop expect files on COM36; keep speaker/audio-output validation separate.

button.next_live_action: No extra proof needed for the basic button event path; new button styles still need focused proof.

checkbox.next_live_action: Add visible selection proof only if UI behavior beyond initial value readback is needed.

combobox.next_live_action: Add dropdown interaction proof separately; cbval.qty is compiled into the fixture but not runtime-readable on this target.

crop-image.next_live_action: Run the new_controls_demo smoke candidate, then add camera proof for the crop-image visual area.

dual-button.next_live_action: Add a focused toggle-state proof if behavior beyond initial value readback is needed.

dualstate-button.next_live_action: Keep runtime distinction from dual-button fixture explicit in any future toggle proof.

external-picture.next_live_action: Commit the live_tft_smoke result JSON with exp0.path, guard.txt, sendme, and camera capture.

gauge.next_live_action: Add camera proof for visible needle/value if visual behavior needs to be closed.

hotspot.next_live_action: Create a hotspot smoke with a fixed printh marker and real touch or proven-equivalent input.

image.next_live_action: Commit a dedicated camera/readback artifact for the packed image smoke if this needs to count as closed.

number.next_live_action: No extra proof needed for the current number readback/increment path.

progress.next_live_action: Add visible value-change camera proof if visual behavior needs to be closed.

qrcode.next_live_action: Add camera proof and decoded payload verification if QR visual behavior needs to be closed.

radio.next_live_action: Add visible selection proof if UI behavior beyond initial value readback is needed.

scrolling-text.next_live_action: Add camera proof over time for actual scrolling movement; keep this separate from unsupported sliding-text.

slider.next_live_action: Add visible value/readback proof for movement if interactive behavior needs to be closed.

state-button.next_live_action: Add focused toggle readback or visible state proof if behavior beyond initial value readback is needed.

text.next_live_action: No extra proof needed for basic text display/readback; font coverage remains its own matrix.

timer.next_live_action: Boot/page-load autorun remains separate; runtime enable/callback is already live-proven.

touch-capture.next_live_action: Create a touch-capture smoke with real touch input and serial/camera evidence.

variable.next_live_action: Add set/get mutation proof if behavior beyond initial value readback is needed.

video.next_live_action: Run the SD-video smoke on COM36 and record live_tft_smoke output; playback quality stays codec-specific.

waveform.next_live_action: Create a waveform smoke that writes sample data and verifies visible drawing or readback.

xfloat.next_live_action: Close xval.vvs0 runtime semantics separately; the committed smoke proves xval.val and xval.vvs1 readback.
